summary: Cathy O'Neil announces her upcoming keynote talk at PyData 2013, titled 'Storytelling With Data.' She notes that the conference schedule incorrectly changed her title to 'Scalable Storytelling With Data,' which she plans to address since her argument is that good tools eliminate the need to obsess over scale. She also mentions an unconventional plan to incorporate an Old Testament story into her Sunday morning presentation.
key_points: The author is delivering a keynote at PyData 2013 at Microsoft's NERD conference center
Her talk title was changed without her input to add 'Scalable' - a change she disagrees with
Her core argument: with great tools, scale becomes less of a concern
She plans to incorporate religious storytelling into a technical talk, finding it fitting for a Sunday morning
money_quotes: One of the points of the talk, in fact, is that with great tools, we don't need to worry too much about the scale.
I'm thinking of addressing this name change in my talk.
It's totally bizarre since I'm not at all religious but for some reason it feels right.
key_insight: Good data tools should liberate us from obsessing over scalability, letting us focus on the storytelling itself.
